subsystem_candidates: LogsTableViewController hosted row/card outer container alignment (estimate-row render path)

supporting_evidence: Top-anchor replay samples show `rowMinYShiftFromStart=0.000` at transition start while `cardOuterMinYShiftFromStart=310.167` and `cardOuterHeightShiftFromStart=-620.333`, indicating a render-local outer-container jump during height collapse.

In the same samples, `cardBorderMinYShiftFromStart=0.000` and `cardTopContainerMinYShiftFromStart=0.000` initially, then `cardVisibleTopEdgeMinY` moves to match `cardOuterMinY`, localizing the divergence to outer-container ownership rather than state-channel promotion.

Incident breadcrumbs show collapse state flags and completion events progressing coherently (`probe.collapse.end`, `probe.topEdge.end`, `animation.complete`), with no evidence of tentative-vs-committed model state mismatch.
contradicting_evidence: No breadcrumb indicates business-rule/policy readers consuming stale committed state; all divergence signals are geometry-owner deltas in the hosted row/card render path.
missing_evidence: Post-fix collapse traces confirming `cardOuterMinYShiftFromStart` remains near 0 during the transient outer-height collapse window while row-local top-edge probes stay top-anchored.
candidates.next_discriminating_check: Apply a narrow top-leading/max-height alignment fix in `LogsTableViewController.swift` for `LogRowHostedView`/`LogRowCardView`, then rerun collapse and verify that `cardOuterMinY` no longer jumps at transition start.

phase_ownership.best_supported_hypothesis: rendered top edge ownership shifts to `cardOuterMinY` because the hosted row/card outer container recenters during transient outer-height collapse
